title: vicia sativa var. cornigera
intro: Vicia sativa var. cornigera, also known as the beaked vetch or beaked hairy vetch, is a variety of the common vetch plant, a type of legume native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The beaked vetch is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. In herbal preparations, the plant's seeds, leaves, and stems are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are valued for their medicinal properties.
